task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'DevOps Support' mailbox is selected or visible in the FreeScout navigation sidebar.
The ticket with the subject 'Production DB Latency' is open in the main viewing area.

task_completion.description: Enter edit mode for the internal note containing the leaked password
task_completion.visual_evidence: The internal note containing the PostgreSQL connection string is open in a text editor within the FreeScout UI.

task_completion.description: Replace the plain-text password with the redaction marker
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the text editor, the password 'ProductionPass2024!' in the connection string is replaced exactly with '[REDACTED]'.

task_completion.description: Save the edited note while preserving the audit trail
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note is saved and visible in the ticket thread showing the redacted connection string (postgres://admin:[REDACTED]@10.0.0.5:5432/prod). The note must still exist in the thread and not be deleted.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with FreeScout
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the FreeScout web interface in a browser, not terminal commands or database queries.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual FreeScout UI to edit the note
integrity.visual_evidence: The redaction is performed using the standard FreeScout note editing interface, not by modifying the DOM via browser developer tools.

integrity.description: The original note was edited, not deleted and recreated
integrity.visual_evidence: The note remains in its original position in the ticket thread (often with an 'edited' indicator if supported by the UI), rather than showing a 'note deleted' event followed by a newly created note.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task takes place in FreeScout. The target mailbox is 'DevOps Support'. The target ticket subject is 'Production DB Latency'. The leaked password to look for is 'ProductionPass2024!' which is part of a PostgreSQL connection string (postgres://admin:ProductionPass2024!@10.0.0.5:5432/prod). The exact redaction marker to replace the password with is '[REDACTED]'. The final connection string should look like postgres://admin:[REDACTED]@10.0.0.5:5432/prod. The note must be edited and saved, not deleted.
